001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.collections;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * A map wrapper that filters entries based on a {@link BiPredicate} when they are added.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * This class wraps an underlying map and applies a filter to determine whether entries should be added.
030 * Only entries that pass the filter (i.e., the predicate returns <jk>true</jk>) are actually stored in
031 * the underlying map.
032 *
033 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
034 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
035 *    <li><b>Flexible Filtering:</b> Use any {@link BiPredicate} to filter entries based on key, value, or both
036 *    <li><b>Optional Filter:</b> Filter is optional when using the builder - defaults to accepting all entries
037 *    <li><b>Multiple Filters:</b> Can combine multiple filters using AND logic
038 *    <li><b>Custom Map Types:</b> Works with any map implementation via the builder's <c>inner</c> method
039 *    <li><b>Type Conversion:</b> Supports automatic type conversion via key/value functions
040 *    <li><b>Convenience Methods:</b> Provides {@link #add(Object, Object)}, {@link #addAll(Map)}, {@link #addAny(Object...)}, and {@link #addPairs(Object...)} for easy entry addition
041 *    <li><b>Transparent Interface:</b> Implements the full {@link Map} interface, so it can be used anywhere a map is expected
042 *    <li><b>Filter on Add:</b> Filtering happens when entries are added via {@link #put(Object, Object)}, {@link #putAll(Map)}, etc.
043 * </ul>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
046 * <p class='bjava'>
047 *    <jc>// Create a filtered map that only accepts non-null values</jc>
048 *    FilteredMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
049 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>)
050 *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk>)
051 *       .build();
052 *
053 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"key1"</js>, <js>"value1"</js>);  <jc>// Added</jc>
054 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"key2"</js>, <jk>null</jk>);      <jc>// Filtered out</jc>
055 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"key3"</js>, <js>"value3"</js>);  <jc>// Added</jc>
056 *
057 *    <jc>// map now contains: {"key1"="value1", "key3"="value3"}</jc>
058 * </p>
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>Example - Custom Map Type:</h5>
061 * <p class='bjava'>
062 *    <jc>// Create a filtered TreeMap that only accepts positive values</jc>
063 *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
064 *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
065 *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
066 *       .inner(<jk>new</jk> TreeMap&lt;&gt;())
067 *       .build();
068 *
069 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"a"</js>, 5);   <jc>// Added</jc>
070 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"b"</js>, -1);  <jc>// Filtered out</jc>
071 *    <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"c"</js>, 10);  <jc>// Added</jc>
072 * </p>
073 *
074 * <h5 class='section'>Behavior Notes:</h5>
075 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
076 *    <li>Filtering is applied to all entry addition methods: {@link #put(Object, Object)}, {@link #putAll(Map)}, etc.
077 *    <li>If the filter returns <jk>false</jk>, the entry is silently ignored (not added)
078 *    <li>When {@link #put(Object, Object)} filters out an entry, it returns the previous value associated with the key
079 *       (if any), or <jk>null</jk> if the key did not exist. This allows callers to distinguish between a new entry
080 *       being filtered out versus an existing entry being filtered out.
081 *    <li>The filter is not applied when reading from the map (e.g., {@link #get(Object)}, {@link #containsKey(Object)})
082 *    <li>All other map operations behave as expected on the underlying map
083 *    <li>The underlying map type is determined by the <c>inner</c> method (defaults to {@link LinkedHashMap})
084 * </ul>
085 *
086 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
087 * <p>
088 * This class is not thread-safe unless the underlying map is thread-safe. If thread safety is required,
089 * use a thread-safe map type (e.g., {@link java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap}) via the <c>inner</c> method.
090 *
091 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
092 * <ul>
093 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="../../../../../index.html#juneau-commons">Overview &gt; juneau-commons</a>
094 * </ul>
095 *
096 * @param <K> The key type.
097 * @param <V> The value type.
098 */
099@SuppressWarnings({
100   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
101})
102public class FilteredMap<K,V> extends AbstractMap<K,V> {
103
104   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
105   private static final String ARG_filter = "filter";
106   private static final String ARG_keyType = "keyType";
107   private static final String ARG_map = "map";
108   private static final String ARG_pairs = "pairs";
109   private static final String ARG_value = "value";
110   private static final String ARG_valueType = "valueType";
111
112   /**
113    * Builder for creating {@link FilteredMap} instances.
114    *
115    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
116    * <p class='bjava'>
117    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
118    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
119    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
120    *       .inner(<jk>new</jk> TreeMap&lt;&gt;())
121    *       .build();
122    * </p>
123    *
124    * @param <K> The key type.
125    * @param <V> The value type.
126    */
127   public static class Builder<K,V> {
128      private BiPredicate<K,V> filter = (k,v) -> true;
129      private Map<K,V> inner;
130      private Class<K> keyType;
131      private Class<V> valueType;
132      private Function<Object,K> keyFunction;
133      private Function<Object,V> valueFunction;
134
135      /**
136       * Sets the filter predicate that determines whether entries should be added.
137       *
138       * <p>
139       * The predicate receives both the key and value of each entry. If it returns <jk>true</jk>,
140       * the entry is added to the map. If it returns <jk>false</jk>, the entry is silently ignored.
141       *
142       * <p>
143       * This method is optional. If not called, the map will accept all entries (defaults to <c>(k,v) -&gt; true</c>).
144       *
145       * <p>
146       * This method can be called multiple times. When called multiple times, all filters are combined
147       * using AND logic - an entry must pass all filters to be added to the map.
148       *
149       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
150       * <p class='bjava'>
151       *    <jc>// Filter out null values</jc>
152       *    Builder&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>b</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>)
153       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk>);
154       *
155       *    <jc>// Filter based on both key and value</jc>
156       *    Builder&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>b2</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
157       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; ! k.startsWith(<js>"_"</js>) &amp;&amp; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0);
158       *
159       *    <jc>// Multiple filters combined with AND</jc>
160       *    Builder&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>b3</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
161       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk>)           <jc>// First filter</jc>
162       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v &gt; 0)                    <jc>// Second filter (ANDed with first)</jc>
163       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; ! k.startsWith(<js>"_"</js>)); <jc>// Third filter (ANDed with previous)</jc>
164       * </p>
165       *
166       * @param value The filter predicate. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
167       * @return This object for method chaining.
168       */
169      public Builder<K,V> filter(BiPredicate<K,V> value) {
170         filter = filter.and(assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value));
171         return this;
172      }
173
174      /**
175       * Sets the underlying map instance that will store the filtered entries.
176       *
177       * <p>
178       * If not specified, a new {@link LinkedHashMap} will be created during {@link #build()}.
179       *
180       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
181       * <p class='bjava'>
182       *    <jc>// Use a TreeMap for sorted keys</jc>
183       *    Builder&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>b</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
184       *       .inner(<jk>new</jk> TreeMap&lt;&gt;());
185       *
186       *    <jc>// Use a ConcurrentHashMap for thread safety</jc>
187       *    Builder&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>b2</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
188       *       .inner(<jk>new</jk> ConcurrentHashMap&lt;&gt;());
189       *
190       *    <jc>// Use an existing map</jc>
191       *    Map&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>existing</jv> = <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;();
192       *    Builder&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>b3</jv> = FilteredMap.<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
193       *       .inner(<jv>existing</jv>);
194       * </p>
195       *
196       * @param value The underlying map instance. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
197       * @return This object for method chaining.
198       */
199      public Builder<K,V> inner(Map<K,V> value) {
200         inner = assertArgNotNull(ARG_value, value);
201         return this;
202      }
203
204      /**
205       * Sets the function to use for converting keys when using {@link FilteredMap#add(Object, Object)}.
206       *
207       * <p>
208       * If specified, keys passed to {@link FilteredMap#add(Object, Object)} will be converted using
209       * this function before being added to the map. If not specified, keys must already be of the
210       * correct type (or <c>Object.class</c> is used if types were not specified, which accepts any type).
211       *
212       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
213       * <p class='bjava'>
214       *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
215       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
216       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk>)
217       *       .keyFunction(o -&gt; o.toString())
218       *       .build();
219       *
220       *    <jv>map</jv>.add(123, 5);  <jc>// Key will be converted from Integer to String</jc>
221       * </p>
222       *
223       * @param value The key conversion function. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
224       * @return This object for method chaining.
225       */
226      public Builder<K,V> keyFunction(Function<Object,K> value) {
227         keyFunction = value;
228         return this;
229      }
230
231      /**
232       * Sets the function to use for converting values when using {@link FilteredMap#add(Object, Object)}.
233       *
234       * <p>
235       * If specified, values passed to {@link FilteredMap#add(Object, Object)} will be converted using
236       * this function before being added to the map. If not specified, values must already be of the
237       * correct type (or <c>Object.class</c> is used if types were not specified, which accepts any type).
238       *
239       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
240       * <p class='bjava'>
241       *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
242       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
243       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
244       *       .valueFunction(o -&gt; Integer.parseInt(o.toString()))
245       *       .build();
246       *
247       *    <jv>map</jv>.add(<js>"key"</js>, <js>"123"</js>);  <jc>// Value will be converted from String to Integer</jc>
248       * </p>
249       *
250       * @param value The value conversion function. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
251       * @return This object for method chaining.
252       */
253      public Builder<K,V> valueFunction(Function<Object,V> value) {
254         valueFunction = value;
255         return this;
256      }
257
258      /**
259       * Sets both the key and value conversion functions at once.
260       *
261       * <p>
262       * This is a convenience method for setting both conversion functions in a single call.
263       *
264       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
265       * <p class='bjava'>
266       *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
267       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
268       *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
269       *       .functions(
270       *          o -&gt; o.toString(),                    <jc>// Key function</jc>
271       *          o -&gt; Integer.parseInt(o.toString())   <jc>// Value function</jc>
272       *       )
273       *       .build();
274       * </p>
275       *
276       * @param keyFunction The key conversion function. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
277       * @param valueFunction The value conversion function. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
278       * @return This object for method chaining.
279       */
280      public Builder<K,V> functions(Function<Object,K> keyFunction, Function<Object,V> valueFunction) {
281         this.keyFunction = keyFunction;
282         this.valueFunction = valueFunction;
283         return this;
284      }
285
286      /**
287       * Builds a new {@link FilteredMap} instance.
288       *
289       * <p>
290       * If {@link #filter(BiPredicate)} was not called, the map will accept all entries
291       * (defaults to <c>(k,v) -&gt; true</c>).
292       *
293       * @return A new filtered map instance.
294       */
295      public FilteredMap<K,V> build() {
296         Map<K,V> map = inner != null ? inner : new LinkedHashMap<>();
297         return new FilteredMap<>(filter, map, keyType, valueType, keyFunction, valueFunction);
298      }
299   }
300
301   /**
302    * Creates a new builder for constructing a filtered map.
303    *
304    * @param <K> The key type.
305    * @param <V> The value type.
306    * @param keyType The key type class. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
307    * @param valueType The value type class. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
308    * @return A new builder.
309    */
310   public static <K,V> Builder<K,V> create(Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType) {
311      assertArgNotNull(ARG_keyType, keyType);
312      assertArgNotNull(ARG_valueType, valueType);
313      var builder = new Builder<K,V>();
314      builder.keyType = keyType;
315      builder.valueType = valueType;
316      return builder;
317   }
318
319   /**
320    * Creates a new builder for constructing a filtered map with generic types.
321    *
322    * <p>
323    * This is a convenience method that creates a builder without requiring explicit type class parameters.
324    * The generic types must be explicitly specified using the diamond operator syntax.
325    *
326    * <p>
327    * When using this method, type checking is effectively disabled (using <c>Object.class</c> as the type),
328    * allowing any key and value types to be added. However, the generic type parameters should still be
329    * specified for type safety.
330    *
331    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
332    * <p class='bjava'>
333    *    <jc>// Explicitly specify generic types using diamond operator</jc>
334    *    <jk>var</jk> <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
335    *       .&lt;String, String&gt;<jsm>create</jsm>()
336    *       .filter((<jv>k</jv>, <jv>v</jv>) -&gt; <jv>v</jv> != <jk>null</jk>)
337    *       .build();
338    * </p>
339    *
340    * @param <K> The key type.
341    * @param <V> The value type.
342    * @return A new builder.
343    */
344   @SuppressWarnings({
345      "unchecked" // Type erasure requires cast to Builder<K,V>
346   })
347   public static <K,V> Builder<K,V> create() {
348      var builder = new Builder<K,V>();
349      builder.keyType = (Class<K>)Object.class;
350      builder.valueType = (Class<V>)Object.class;
351      return builder;
352   }
353   private final BiPredicate<K,V> filter;
354   private final Map<K,V> map;
355   private final Class<K> keyType;
356   private final Class<V> valueType;
357   private final Function<Object,K> keyFunction;
358   private final Function<Object,V> valueFunction;
359
360   /**
361    * Constructor.
362    *
363    * @param filter The filter predicate. Can be <jk>null</jk> (if null, all entries are accepted).
364    * @param map The underlying map. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
365    * @param keyType The key type. Must not be <jk>null</jk> (use <c>Object.class</c> to disable type checking).
366    * @param valueType The value type. Must not be <jk>null</jk> (use <c>Object.class</c> to disable type checking).
367    * @param keyFunction The key conversion function, or <jk>null</jk> if not specified.
368    * @param valueFunction The value conversion function, or <jk>null</jk> if not specified.
369    */
370   protected FilteredMap(BiPredicate<K,V> filter, Map<K,V> map, Class<K> keyType, Class<V> valueType, Function<Object,K> keyFunction, Function<Object,V> valueFunction) {
371      this.filter = assertArgNotNull(ARG_filter, filter);
372      this.map = assertArgNotNull(ARG_map, map);
373      this.keyType = assertArgNotNull(ARG_keyType, keyType);
374      this.valueType = assertArgNotNull(ARG_valueType, valueType);
375      this.keyFunction = keyFunction;
376      this.valueFunction = valueFunction;
377   }
378
379   /**
380    * Associates the specified value with the specified key in this map, if the entry passes the filter.
381    *
382    * <p>
383    * If the entry passes the filter, it is added to the map and this method returns the previous value
384    * associated with the key (or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping).
385    *
386    * <p>
387    * If the entry is filtered out, it is not added to the map. This method returns the previous value
388    * associated with the key (or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping). This allows callers to distinguish
389    * between a new entry being filtered out versus an existing entry being filtered out.
390    *
391    * @param key The key with which the specified value is to be associated.
392    * @param value The value to be associated with the specified key.
393    * @return The previous value associated with <c>key</c>, or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping for <c>key</c>.
394    *    This applies whether the entry was added or filtered out.
395    */
396   @Override
397   public V put(K key, V value) {
398      if (filter.test(key, value))
399         return map.put(key, value);
400      return map.get(key);  // Return previous value if exists, null otherwise
401   }
402
403   @Override
404   public void putAll(Map<? extends K, ? extends V> m) {
405      for (var entry : m.entrySet()) {
406         if (filter.test(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue()))
407            map.put(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue());
408      }
409   }
410
411   /**
412    * Tests whether the specified key-value pair would pass the filter and be added to this map.
413    *
414    * <p>
415    * This method can be used to check if an entry would be accepted by the filter without actually
416    * adding it to the map. This is useful for validation, debugging, or pre-checking entries before
417    * attempting to add them.
418    *
419    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
420    * <p class='bjava'>
421    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
422    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
423    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
424    *       .build();
425    *
426    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>map</jv>.wouldAccept(<js>"key"</js>, 5)) {
427    *       <jv>map</jv>.put(<js>"key"</js>, 5);  <jc>// Will be added</jc>
428    *    }
429    * </p>
430    *
431    * @param key The key to test.
432    * @param value The value to test.
433    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the entry would be added to the map, <jk>false</jk> if it would be filtered out.
434    */
435   public boolean wouldAccept(K key, V value) {
436      return filter.test(key, value);
437   }
438
439   /**
440    * Returns the filter predicate used by this map.
441    *
442    * <p>
443    * This method provides access to the filter for debugging, inspection, or advanced use cases.
444    * The returned predicate is the combined filter used internally by this map. If multiple filters
445    * were set via the builder, they are combined using AND logic.
446    *
447    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
448    * <p class='bjava'>
449    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
450    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
451    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
452    *       .build();
453    *
454    *    BiPredicate&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>filter</jv> = <jv>map</jv>.getFilter();
455    *    <jc>// Use filter for other purposes</jc>
456    * </p>
457    *
458    * @return The filter predicate. Never <jk>null</jk>.
459    */
460   public BiPredicate<K,V> getFilter() {
461      return filter;
462   }
463
464   @Override
465   public Set<Entry<K,V>> entrySet() {
466      return map.entrySet();
467   }
468
469   @Override
470   public V get(Object key) {
471      return map.get(key);
472   }
473
474   @Override
475   public V remove(Object key) {
476      return map.remove(key);
477   }
478
479   @Override
480   public boolean containsKey(Object key) {
481      return map.containsKey(key);
482   }
483
484   @Override
485   public boolean containsValue(Object value) {
486      return map.containsValue(value);
487   }
488
489   @Override
490   public int size() {
491      return map.size();
492   }
493
494   @Override
495   public boolean isEmpty() {
496      return map.isEmpty();
497   }
498
499   @Override
500   public void clear() {
501      map.clear();
502   }
503
504   @Override
505   public Set<K> keySet() {
506      return map.keySet();
507   }
508
509   @Override
510   public Collection<V> values() {
511      return map.values();
512   }
513
514   /**
515    * Adds an entry to this map with automatic type conversion.
516    *
517    * <p>
518    * This method converts the key and value using the configured key/value functions (if any) and validates
519    * the types (if key/value types were specified when creating the map). After conversion and validation,
520    * the entry is added using the standard {@link #put(Object, Object)} method, which applies the filter.
521    *
522    * <h5 class='section'>Type Conversion:</h5>
523    * <ul>
524    *    <li>If a key function is configured, the key is converted by applying the function
525    *    <li>If a value function is configured, the value is converted by applying the function
526    *    <li>If no function is configured, the object is used as-is (but may be validated if types were specified)
527    * </ul>
528    *
529    * <h5 class='section'>Type Validation:</h5>
530    * <ul>
531    *    <li>If key type was specified (via {@link #create(Class, Class)}), the converted key must be an instance of that type
532    *    <li>If value type was specified (via {@link #create(Class, Class)}), the converted value must be an instance of that type
533    *    <li>If {@link #create()} was used (no types specified), <c>Object.class</c> is used, which accepts any type
534    * </ul>
535    *
536    * <h5 class='section'>Return Value:</h5>
537    * <ul>
538    *    <li>If the entry is added successfully, returns the previous value associated with the key (or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping)
539    *    <li>If the entry is filtered out, returns <jk>null</jk> (even if there was a previous value)
540    * </ul>
541    *
542    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
543    * <p class='bjava'>
544    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
545    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
546    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
547    *       .valueFunction(o -&gt; Integer.parseInt(o.toString()))
548    *       .build();
549    *
550    *    <jv>map</jv>.add(<js>"key"</js>, <js>"123"</js>);  <jc>// Value converted from String to Integer</jc>
551    * </p>
552    *
553    * @param key The key to add. Will be converted if a key function is configured.
554    * @param value The value to add. Will be converted if a value function is configured.
555    * @return The previous value associated with the key if the entry was added, or <jk>null</jk> if there was no mapping or the entry was filtered out.
556    * @throws RuntimeException If conversion fails or type validation fails.
557    */
558   public V add(Object key, Object value) {
559      K convertedKey = convertKey(key);
560      V convertedValue = convertValue(value);
561      if (filter.test(convertedKey, convertedValue))
562         return put(convertedKey, convertedValue);
563      return null;  // Filtered out, return null
564   }
565
566   /**
567    * Adds all entries from the specified map with automatic type conversion.
568    *
569    * <p>
570    * This method iterates over all entries in the source map and adds each one using {@link #add(Object, Object)},
571    * which applies conversion and validation before adding.
572    *
573    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
574    * <p class='bjava'>
575    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
576    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
577    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
578    *       .valueFunction(o -&gt; Integer.parseInt(o.toString()))
579    *       .build();
580    *
581    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>source</jv> = Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"5"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"-1"</js>);
582    *    <jv>map</jv>.addAll(<jv>source</jv>);  <jc>// Values converted, negative value filtered out</jc>
583    * </p>
584    *
585    * @param source The map containing entries to add. Can be <jk>null</jk> (no-op).
586    * @return This object for method chaining.
587    */
588   public FilteredMap<K,V> addAll(Map<?,?> source) {
589      if (source != null) {
590         for (var entry : source.entrySet()) {
591            add(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue());
592         }
593      }
594      return this;
595   }
596
597   /**
598    * Adds arbitrary values to this map.
599    *
600    * <p>
601    * Objects can be any of the following:
602    * <ul>
603    *    <li>Maps of key/value types convertible to the key/value types of this map.
604    * </ul>
605    *
606    * <p>
607    * Each entry from the maps will be added using {@link #add(Object, Object)}, which applies
608    * conversion and filtering.
609    *
610    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
611    * <p class='bjava'>
612    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
613    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
614    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
615    *       .build();
616    *
617    *    Map&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, 5, <js>"b"</js>, -1);
618    *    Map&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = Map.of(<js>"c"</js>, 10);
619    *    <jv>map</jv>.addAny(<jv>map1</jv>, <jv>map2</jv>);  <jc>// Adds a=5, c=10 (b=-1 filtered out)</jc>
620    * </p>
621    *
622    * @param values The values to add. Can be <jk>null</jk> or contain <jk>null</jk> values (ignored).
623    * @return This object for method chaining.
624    */
625   public FilteredMap<K,V> addAny(Object...values) {
626      if (values != null) {
627         for (var o : values) {
628            if (o != null && o instanceof Map<?,?> m) {
629               addAll(m);
630            }
631         }
632      }
633      return this;
634   }
635
636   /**
637    * Adds a list of key/value pairs to this map.
638    *
639    * <p>
640    * The pairs are processed in order, with each pair consisting of two consecutive elements
641    * in the array. Each pair is added using {@link #add(Object, Object)}, which applies
642    * conversion and filtering.
643    *
644    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
645    * <p class='bjava'>
646    *    FilteredMap&lt;String, Integer&gt; <jv>map</jv> = FilteredMap
647    *       .<jsm>create</jsm>(String.<jk>class</jk>, Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
648    *       .filter((k, v) -&gt; v != <jk>null</jk> &amp;&amp; v &gt; 0)
649    *       .build();
650    *
651    *    <jv>map</jv>.addPairs(<js>"a"</js>, 5, <js>"b"</js>, -1, <js>"c"</js>, 10);
652    *    <jc>// Adds a=5, c=10 (b=-1 filtered out)</jc>
653    * </p>
654    *
655    * @param pairs The pairs to add. Must have an even number of elements.
656    * @return This object for method chaining.
657    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If an odd number of parameters is provided.
658    */
659   public FilteredMap<K,V> addPairs(Object...pairs) {
660      assertArgNotNull(ARG_pairs, pairs);
661      if (pairs.length % 2 != 0)
662         throw iaex("Odd number of parameters passed into addPairs()");
663      for (var i = 0; i < pairs.length; i += 2)
664         add(pairs[i], pairs[i + 1]);
665      return this;
666   }
667
668   private K convertKey(Object key) {
669      if (keyFunction != null) {
670         key = keyFunction.apply(key);
671      }
672      if (key == null) {
673         // Allow null for non-primitive types
674         if (keyType.isPrimitive())
675            throw rex("Cannot set null key for primitive type {0}", keyType.getName());
676         return null;
677      }
678      if (keyType.isInstance(key))
679         return keyType.cast(key);
680      throw rex("Object of type {0} could not be converted to key type {1}", cn(key), cn(keyType));
681   }
682
683   private V convertValue(Object value) {
684      if (valueFunction != null) {
685         value = valueFunction.apply(value);
686      }
687      if (value == null) {
688         // Allow null for non-primitive types
689         if (valueType.isPrimitive())
690            throw rex("Cannot set null value for primitive type {0}", valueType.getName());
691         return null;
692      }
693      if (valueType.isInstance(value))
694         return valueType.cast(value);
695      throw rex("Object of type {0} could not be converted to value type {1}", cn(value), cn(valueType));
696   }
697
698   @Override
699   public String toString() {
700      return map.toString();
701   }
702
703   @Override
704   public boolean equals(Object o) {
705      return map.equals(o);
706   }
707
708   @Override
709   public int hashCode() {
710      return map.hashCode();
711   }
712}
713
